title: RCX Registry — Passport Publish v1.0
description: Publishable descriptor of an agent passport. Carries identity, sponsor lineage, reputation tier, and capability declarations — but never private key material. Designed to be discoverable across the RCX ecosystem so other agents/orgs can resolve a passport and verify its lineage. Sourced from a Crux Daemon's `__passport__::{id}` fact via `corecruxctl publish passport <id>`.

publisher_passport.description: Daemon-root passport that signed the publish event (BLAKE3 fingerprint, p_<32 hex>).

passport_fpr.description: Fingerprint of the passport being published. Identical format to publisher_passport.

passport_id.description: Operator-chosen slug (e.g. 'personal-default', 'work-team-alpha'). Lowercase alphanumeric, with hyphens/underscores.

category.description: Tenant-category convention. Filterable so other agents can find passports of a given posture.

public_key_hex.description: Lowercase hex-encoded ed25519 verifying key (32 bytes → 64 hex chars).

sponsor_passport_fpr.description: Optional sponsor lineage. Points at the parent passport that vouched for this one.

reputation_tier.description: Five-tier reputation derived from local receipt count. Sync gates may filter on this.

receipt_count.description: Number of receipted operations the passport has accrued at publish time. Drives reputation_tier.

agent_work_gate.description: When true, the passport's owner has opted into human-in-the-loop on agent state changes. Other orgs can filter for 'agents that pause for review' vs. 'agents that act autonomously'.

is_default_for_category.description: Whether the source daemon treats this as the default passport for its category.

operator_metadata.description: Optional opaque metadata block. Free-form JSON the operator can attach. The registry MAY ignore unknown fields.

issued_at.description: When the passport was first issued on its source daemon (RFC 3339).

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over the canonical CBOR encoding of all preceding fields (passport_hash). Hex-encoded.

signer_kid.description: Key id of the signer — for the passport-publish flow, equals the publisher_passport's fingerprint.

passport_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of the canonical CBOR encoding (32 bytes → 64 hex chars). Drives signature integrity.
